summary: Matched the first slice of Sheik's SpecialHi/Up-B source, replacing placeholder address-range comments in `ftSk_SpecialHi.c` with six fully matching functions and related includes/prototypes. The PR report shows the file at 15.75% matched, with `fn_80112ED8`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_80112F48`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_80112FA8`, `fn_80113038`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_Enter`, and `ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Enter` all at 100%. It also renamed one Sheik attribute field from `x2C` to `self_vel_y`, removed a scratch-link comment from `ftSk_SpecialN.c`, and the diff records deletion of the large `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialS.s` assembly file.

key_files.role: Main decomp target; gained includes, local prototypes, and six matched Sheik SpecialHi functions.
Renamed `ftSeakAttributes.x2C` to `self_vel_y` based on use in aerial SpecialHi enter logic.
Removed a decomp.me scratch URL comment from an already decompiled function.
Diff shows the entire 4979-line assembly file was deleted; the excerpt does not explain the relationship to the SpecialHi matching work.

what_changed: Added concrete implementations for `fn_80112ED8`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_80112F48`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_80112FA8`, `fn_80113038`, `ftSk_SpecialHi_Enter`, and `ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Enter` in `ftSk_SpecialHi.c`.

Replaced placeholder address-range comments for the first SpecialHi functions with C code using `GET_FIGHTER`, fighter callbacks, effect spawning, item spawning, motion-state changes, and animation setup.
`fn_80112ED8` checks `fp->x2219_b0`, calls the vanish item/effect helpers when not already set, plays an effect/sound-like event through `ft_80088148(fp, 115, 127, 64)`, and clears `accessory4_cb`.
`ftSk_SpecialHi_80112F48` reads the hip joint position with `lb_8000B1CC`, forces `pos.z = 0`, and calls `it_802B1C60(gobj, &pos, fp->facing_dir)`.

`ftSk_SpecialHi_80112FA8` and `fn_80113038` spawn effects `1284` and `1285` respectively via `efSync_Spawn`, guard spawning with `fp->x2219_b0`, and assign `pre_hitlag_cb = efLib_PauseAll` and `post_hitlag_cb = efLib_ResumeAll`.
`ftSk_SpecialHi_Enter` initializes `cmd_vars[0]` and `fp->mv.sk.specialhi.xC`, changes to `ftSk_MS_SpecialHiStart_0`, and calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.
`ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Enter` does the same start-state setup for `ftSk_MS_SpecialAirHiStart_0` and additionally sets `fp->self_vel.y` from `((ftSeakAttributes*) fp->dat_attrs)->self_vel_y`.
Added necessary includes for fighter forward declarations, Sheik forward declarations, effects, fighter types/inlines, Sheik vanish item code, joint-position helper code, and `baselib/gobj.h`.
Changed `ftSeakAttributes.x2C` from an anonymous `s32` to `float self_vel_y`, matching observed use as an aerial Up-B vertical velocity.
Deleted `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialS.s` according to the diff, though the provided context gives weak evidence for why this was paired with a SpecialHi C match.
smart_moves: Used behavior-specific evidence to name `ftSeakAttributes.self_vel_y`: the field is loaded from `fp->dat_attrs` and written directly into `fp->self_vel.y` in `ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Enter`.
Kept unknown local functions address-based (`fn_80112ED8`, `fn_80113038`) while assigning descriptive names only where the existing SpecialHi naming pattern supported it.
Preserved address comments in static prototypes (`/* 112F48 */`, `/* 112FA8 */`, `/* 113038 */`), making it easier to correlate matched C with the original symbol/order.

Matched small lifecycle helpers first: enter functions, effect/item spawn helpers, and callback cleanup code are compact and yielded six 100% functions, improving file progress without overreaching on larger routines.
Modeled hitlag behavior explicitly by assigning effect pause/resume callbacks (`efLib_PauseAll`, `efLib_ResumeAll`) after effect spawning.
Used hip joint world position via `fp->parts[FtPart_HipN].joint` and `lb_8000B1CC`, a reusable pattern for character-centered item/effect spawns.
decomp_lessons: For fighter special-move enter functions, check for repeated boilerplate: zero `cmd_vars[0]`, clear move-specific state, call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, then call `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.
When a dat attribute is immediately assigned to a strongly named fighter field, it can justify a typed float rename even if the original struct field was anonymous.

Effect-spawning helper functions often guard with a fighter flag such as `x2219_b0`, set the flag after spawning, and install `pre_hitlag_cb`/`post_hitlag_cb` so effects pause and resume correctly during hitlag.
Joint-position helpers (`lb_8000B1CC` with `FtPart_HipN`) are useful anchors for matching item/effect spawn code in special moves.
Small dummy local arrays like `u8 _[4]` and `u8 _[20]` may be needed to preserve stack layout and match codegen for short fighter functions.

Do not remove or rename anonymous function symbols unless the behavior or existing project naming pattern supports a clearer name; this PR leaves `fn_80112ED8` and `fn_80113038` as address-style names.
Cleaning stray scratch links from source comments is consistent with keeping decomp source focused on checked-in evidence rather than temporary external workspaces.
naming_conventions: `ftSk_SpecialHi_Enter` and `ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Enter` follow the fighter/action naming convention for ground and air special enter functions.
`ftSk_SpecialHi_80112F48` and `ftSk_SpecialHi_80112FA8` use a subsystem prefix plus address suffix for behavior-known but not fully semantically named helpers.
`fn_80112ED8` and `fn_80113038` retain generic `fn_` address-based names where the provided evidence does not justify a descriptive name.
`ftSeakAttributes.self_vel_y` uses a semantic field name tied to its direct assignment into `fp->self_vel.y`.
Motion-state constants used include `ftSk_MS_SpecialHiStart_0` and `ftSk_MS_SpecialAirHiStart_0`, preserving the existing Sheik motion-state naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched six short functions at 100% according to the PR report, raising `ftSk_SpecialHi.c` to 15.75% matched overall.
Used exact callback assignments and branch guards around `fp->x2219_b0` to mirror one-shot effect/item spawning behavior.
Inserted explicit stack-padding locals (`u8 _[4]`, `u8 _[20]`) to influence stack frame layout and achieve matching codegen.

Used typed forward headers and concrete includes rather than relying only on `<placeholder.h>`, enabling calls to `efSync_Spawn`, `efLib_PauseAll`, `efLib_ResumeAll`, `it_802B1C60`, `lb_8000B1CC`, and fighter APIs.
The diff deletes a 4979-line `ftSk_SpecialS.s` asm file; the slice does not provide enough evidence to state whether this deletion was a deliberate asm-removal milestone or an incidental cleanup.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
Because there was no reviewer text, there is no direct evidence of requested naming, header, or matching changes.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialHi.c` for semantic names replacing `fn_80112ED8` or `fn_80113038`.
Check subsequent changes to `ftSeakAttributes` to confirm neighboring fields around `self_vel_y` and whether more SpecialHi attributes were named.
Investigate why `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialS.s` was deleted in a SpecialHi PR, since this slice does not explain that relationship.
Search for similar `efSync_Spawn` plus `efLib_PauseAll`/`efLib_ResumeAll` patterns in other fighter vanish/teleport specials.
Compare other fighter enter functions that use `u8 _[20]` stack padding around `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` for matching tactics.
